speaker: HAMLET
text: The air bites shrewdly; it is very cold.

speaker: HORATIO
text: It is a nipping and an eager air.

speaker: HAMLET
text: What hour now?

speaker: HORATIO
text: I think it lacks of twelve.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: No, it is struck.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Indeed, I heard it not. It then draws near the season

speaker: HORATIO
text: Wherein the spirit held his wont to walk.

speaker: HORATIO
text: What does this mean, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: The King doth wake tonight and takes his rouse,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Keeps wassail, and the swagg’ring upspring reels;

speaker: HAMLET
text: And, as he drains his draughts of Rhenish down,

speaker: HAMLET
text: The kettledrum and trumpet thus bray out

speaker: HAMLET
text: The triumph of his pledge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Is it a custom?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ay, marry, is ’t,

speaker: HAMLET
text: But, to my mind, though I am native here

speaker: HAMLET
text: And to the manner born, it is a custom

speaker: HAMLET
text: More honored in the breach than the observance.

speaker: HAMLET
text: This heavy-headed revel east and west

speaker: HAMLET
text: Makes us traduced and taxed of other nations.

speaker: HAMLET
text: They clepe us drunkards and with swinish phrase

speaker: HAMLET
text: Soil our addition. And, indeed, it takes

speaker: HAMLET
text: From our achievements, though performed at height,

speaker: HAMLET
text: The pith and marrow of our attribute.

speaker: HAMLET
text: So oft it chances in particular men

speaker: HAMLET
text: That for some vicious mole of nature in them,

speaker: HAMLET
text: As in their birth (wherein they are not guilty,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Since nature cannot choose his origin),

speaker: HAMLET
text: By the o’ergrowth of some complexion

speaker: HAMLET
text: (Oft breaking down the pales and forts of reason),

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or by some habit that too much o’erleavens

speaker: HAMLET
text: The form of plausive manners—that these men,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Carrying, I say, the stamp of one defect,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Being nature’s livery or fortune’s star,

speaker: HAMLET
text: His virtues else, be they as pure as grace,

speaker: HAMLET
text: As infinite as man may undergo,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Shall in the general censure take corruption

speaker: HAMLET
text: From that particular fault. The dram of evil

speaker: HAMLET
text: Doth all the noble substance of a doubt

speaker: HAMLET
text: To his own scandal.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Look, my lord, it comes.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Angels and ministers of grace, defend us!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Be thou a spirit of health or goblin damned,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Bring with thee airs from heaven or blasts from hell,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Be thy intents wicked or charitable,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Thou com’st in such a questionable shape

speaker: HAMLET
text: That I will speak to thee. I’ll call thee “Hamlet,”

speaker: HAMLET
text: “King,” “Father,” “Royal Dane.” O, answer me!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Let me not burst in ignorance, but tell

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why thy canonized bones, hearsèd in death,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Have burst their cerements; why the sepulcher,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Wherein we saw thee quietly interred,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Hath oped his ponderous and marble jaws

speaker: HAMLET
text: To cast thee up again. What may this mean

speaker: HAMLET
text: That thou, dead corse, again in complete steel,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Revisits thus the glimpses of the moon,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Making night hideous, and we fools of nature

speaker: HAMLET
text: So horridly to shake our disposition

speaker: HAMLET
text: With thoughts beyond the reaches of our souls?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Say, why is this? Wherefore? What should we do?

speaker: HORATIO
text: It beckons you to go away with it

speaker: HORATIO
text: As if it some impartment did desire

speaker: HORATIO
text: To you alone.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: Look with what courteous action

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: It waves you to a more removèd ground.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: But do not go with it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: No, by no means.

speaker: HAMLET
text: It will not speak. Then I will follow it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Do not, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, what should be the fear?

speaker: HAMLET
text: I do not set my life at a pin’s fee.

speaker: HAMLET
text: And for my soul, what can it do to that,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Being a thing immortal as itself?

speaker: HAMLET
text: It waves me forth again. I’ll follow it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: What if it tempt you toward the flood, my lord?

speaker: HORATIO
text: Or to the dreadful summit of the cliff

speaker: HORATIO
text: That beetles o’er his base into the sea,

speaker: HORATIO
text: And there assume some other horrible form

speaker: HORATIO
text: Which might deprive your sovereignty of reason

speaker: HORATIO
text: And draw you into madness? Think of it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: The very place puts toys of desperation,

speaker: HORATIO
text: Without more motive, into every brain

speaker: HORATIO
text: That looks so many fathoms to the sea

speaker: HORATIO
text: And hears it roar beneath.

speaker: HAMLET
text: It waves me still.—Go on, I’ll follow thee.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: You shall not go, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Hold off your hands.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Be ruled. You shall not go.

speaker: HAMLET
text: My fate cries out

speaker: HAMLET
text: And makes each petty arture in this body

speaker: HAMLET
text: As hardy as the Nemean lion’s nerve.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Still am I called. Unhand me, gentlemen.

speaker: HAMLET
text: By heaven, I’ll make a ghost of him that lets me!

speaker: HAMLET
text: I say, away!—Go on. I’ll follow thee.

speaker: HORATIO
text: He waxes desperate with imagination.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: Let’s follow. ’Tis not fit thus to obey him.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Have after. To what issue will this come?

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: Something is rotten in the state of Denmark.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Heaven will direct it.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: Nay, let’s follow him.
